title: ReFormer JSON form schema (M1, string-operator DSL)
description: Base meta-schema: validates node structure + operator string syntax ($model/$component/$dataSource). Component-name enum is tightened per-app via buildFormSchemaMetaSchema; $dataSource names and $model paths are checked outside this schema.

modelOp.description: Model binding: "$model(path)" — path resolved at runtime (not validated here).

componentOp.description: Registry component: "$component(Name)". Name is tightened to a registry enum by buildFormSchemaMetaSchema.

htmlOp.description: Native HTML tag: "$html(div)". The tag is checked against the presentational whitelist (isAllowedHtmlTag) by validateFormSchema and by the converter; buildFormSchemaMetaSchema tightens it to an enum for IDE completion.

textContent.description: Text content of a node: a literal, an operator string ("$model(path)" for reactive model values, "$locale(key)" for localized strings), or an array of parts joined without a separator.
textContent.$comment: Types are listed as separate anyOf branches rather than a union "type": ["string","number"] — ajv's strict mode rejects union type keywords unless allowUnionTypes is on, and validateFormSchema compiles this schema without it.

operatorOp.description: Any DSL operator string ("$model/$component/$dataSource/$fn/$locale(...)") used as an escape hatch on a componentProps value (e.g. "options": "$dataSource(LOAN_TYPES)"). The component-props validator wraps every prop in anyOf:[honest type, operatorOp] via allowOperatorStrings, so an operator string is accepted wherever a literal value is.

node.$comment: Discriminated by which operator key is present (array→value→component), mirroring the isArrayNode/isFieldNode/isContainerNode runtime guards. Using oneOf here made ajv (allErrors) report failures against all three branches for any single mistake, burying the real cause; if/then/else surfaces only the relevant branch's errors.
